Verified from verifier.py which uses 24 as the minimum frame count threshold. The source dwanko_run.tnz contains 72 frames at 24fps, meaning a minimum of 24 frames represents 1 second of animation.
Verified from verifier.py and export_result.sh logic, which uses exactly this percentage (15% of width) as the minimum horizontal centroid shift required to pass the movement verification.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires animating a column pan for the 'dwanko_run' character in OpenToonz. The source scene file is located at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz' and originates from the official, non-synthetic OpenToonz sample repository. The required output resolution is 1280x720, saving as a PNG sequence to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/column_pan/'. The verifier confirms task success by checking that at least 24 frames are rendered and that the horizontal centroid of non-transparent pixels shifts by a minimum of 15% of the frame width (192 pixels) from the first to the last rendered frame.
